    "Evacuation decay test failed",
    "Pressure rose rapidly from 353 microns to 4857 microns in 10 minutes",
    "Indicates a significant system leak rather than simple moisture outgassing"
  ],

    "Pressurize the system with dry nitrogen",
    "Perform leak detection using soap bubbles or an electronic leak detector",
    "Repair the identified leak, then re-evacuate and re-test"

  "rationale": "A rise from 353 microns to 4857 microns in 10 minutes is a rapid, continuous decay that far exceeds the standard 500-micron threshold for a successful standing vacuum test. Because the pressure rose well past 1000-1500 microns and continues to climb rapidly, it indicates a physical atmospheric leak in the system rather than moisture outgassing.",

    "EPA Section 608",
    "Industry Standard Evacuation Practices (500 micron hold)"
  ]
